blueprint.problem_statement: The EU lacks a dedicated regulatory framework for quantum technologies, resulting in fragmented investment, unclear standardisation, and insufficient coordination to compete with US and Chinese quantum programmes.
blueprint.baseline_description: Current state: EU Quantum Flagship running, ~320 CORDIS-funded quantum projects, GERD at ~2.22% GDP. No dedicated quantum-specific regulation or certification framework exists.

objectives.description: Strengthen EU strategic autonomy in quantum technologies
Increase coordinated EU public investment in quantum R&D
Establish EU-wide quantum certification and standardisation
Build a skilled quantum workforce across Member States

policy_options.name: Baseline — no new intervention
policy_options.description: Maintain current trajectory. No new EU-level quantum regulation. Quantum Flagship continues under existing rules.

policy_options.name: Coordination only — voluntary frameworks
policy_options.description: Soft coordination: voluntary funding alignment, talent mobility schemes, and a non-binding quantum roadmap. No mandatory requirements.

policy_options.name: Light regulation — standardisation and certification
policy_options.description: Mandatory EU quantum standards (via ETSI/CEN), security certification for quantum communications, and energy-efficiency requirements for quantum hardware.

policy_options.name: Comprehensive regulation — EU Quantum Agency
policy_options.description: Dedicated EU Quantum Agency, mandatory certification for all quantum systems used in critical infrastructure, EU procurement preference, and binding investment targets.

constraints.note: Empty indicators list triggers live Eurostat + CORDIS fetch
